transcript.chinese: 帕特里克·温斯顿：《统一军事司法法典》规定，任何派遣士兵不带武器上战场的军官都应被送上军事法庭。
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: The Uniform Code of Military Justice specifies court martial for any officer who sends a soldier into battle without a weapon.

transcript.chinese: 对学生也应该有类似的保护，因为学生不应该在没有沟通能力的情况下进入生活，那是因为你人生的成功很大程度上取决于你的说话能力，
transcript.english: There ought to be a similar protection for students because students shouldn't go out into life without the ability to communicate, and that's because your success in life will be determined largely by your ability to speak,

transcript.chinese: 你的写作能力，以及你的想法的质量，按这个顺序。
transcript.english: your ability to write, and the quality of your ideas, in that order.

transcript.chinese: 我知道我能在这方面取得成功，因为沟通的质量、你的演讲、你的写作，很大程度上是由这个公式决定的。
transcript.english: I know that I can be successful in this because the quality of communication, your speaking, your writing, is largely determined by this formula.

transcript.chinese: 这取决于你拥有多少知识，你用这些知识进行了多少练习，以及你与生俱来的天赋，请注意，T 非常小。
transcript.english: It's a matter of how much knowledge you have, how much you practice with that knowledge, and your inherent talent, and notice that the T is very small.

transcript.chinese: 真正重要的是你所知道的。
transcript.english: What really matters is what you know.

transcript.chinese: 几十年前，当我在太阳谷滑雪时，我突然想到了这一点。
transcript.english: This point came to me suddenly a few decades ago when I was skiing at Sun Valley.

transcript.chinese: 我听说那是名人周末，其中一位名人是著名的奥林匹克体操运动员玛丽·卢·雷顿，她在跳马中取得了完美的十多岁成绩。
transcript.english: I had heard that it was Celebrity Weekend, and one of the celebrities was Mary Lou Retton, famous Olympic gymnast, perfect 10s in the vault.

transcript.chinese: 而且听说她是滑雪新手，
transcript.english: And I heard that she was a novice at skiing,

transcript.chinese: 所以当时机到来时，我向新手坡望去，看到了这个年轻女子，当她失去平衡时，就变成了那样。
transcript.english: so when the opportune moment arrived, I looked over on the novice slope and saw this young woman who, when she became unbalanced, went like that.

transcript.chinese: 我说那一定是她。
transcript.english: And I said that's got to be her.

transcript.chinese: 那一定是体操运动员。
transcript.english: That must be the gymnast.

transcript.chinese: 但后来，我突然想到，我的滑雪技术比她好得多，而她是一名奥林匹克运动员——不仅仅是一名普通的奥林匹克运动员，而是一名杰出的运动员。
transcript.english: But then, it occurred to me, I'm a much better skier than she is, and she's an Olympic athlete-- not only an ordinary Olympic athlete, an outstanding one.

transcript.chinese: 我是一个更好的滑雪者，因为我有 K，我有 P，而她只有 T。
transcript.english: And I was a better skier because I had the K, and I had the P, and all she had was the T.

transcript.chinese: 因此，如果您具有
transcript.english: So you can get a lot better than people who may have inherent talents if you have

transcript.chinese: ，那么您可以比那些可能具有天生才能的人做得更好。适量的知识。
transcript.english: the right amount of knowledge.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我今天的目标，这就是我的承诺。
transcript.english: So that's what my objective is today, and here's my promise.

transcript.chinese: 今天，你会看到一些例子，说明你可以将哪些内容放入你的演讲技巧武器库中，而这些例子中的某些例子，一些启发式的例子，一些技巧，也许只有一种，将会使你获得这份工作。
transcript.english: Today, you will see some examples of what you can put in your armamentarium of speaking techniques, and it will be the case that some one of those examples, some heuristic, some technique, maybe only one, will make-- will be the one that gets you the job.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是一个非常非线性的过程。
transcript.english: And so this is a very non-linear process.

transcript.chinese: 你永远不知道什么时候会发生，但这是我的承诺。
transcript.english: You never know when it's going to happen, but that is my promise.

transcript.chinese: 在接下来的 60 分钟结束时，您将接触到很多想法，其中一些您将融入到您自己的技能中，并且它们将确保您获得最大的机会让您的想法被与您交谈的人重视和接受。
transcript.english: By the end of the next 60 minutes, you'll have been exposed to a lot of ideas, some of which you'll incorporate into your own repertoire, and they will ensure that you get the maximum opportunity to have your ideas valued and accepted by the people you speak with.

transcript.chinese: 现在，为了做到这一点，我们必须制定参与规则，并且
transcript.english: Now, in order to do that, we have to have a rule of engagement, and that

transcript.chinese: 就是没有笔记本电脑，没有手机。
transcript.english: is no laptops, no cell phones.

transcript.chinese: 所以，如果你可以关闭它们，我会在你完成后立即开始。
transcript.english: So if you could close those, I'll start up as soon as you're done.

transcript.chinese: 有些人问为什么这是一条参与规则，答案是，我们人类只有一种语言处理器。
transcript.english: Some people ask why that is a rule of engagement, and the answer is, we humans only have one language processor.

transcript.chinese: 如果您的语言处理器工作正常，请您关闭笔记本电脑好吗？
transcript.english: And if your language processor is enga-- could you shut the laptop, please?

transcript.chinese: 如果您的语言处理器正在忙于浏览网页或阅读电子邮件，那么您就会分心。
transcript.english: If your language processor is engaged browsing the web or reading your email, you're distracted.

transcript.chinese: 更糟糕的是，你会分散周围所有人的注意力。
transcript.english: And worse yet, you distract all of the people around you.

transcript.chinese: 研究表明。
transcript.english: Studies have shown that.

transcript.chinese: 更糟糕的是，如果我在后面或这里看到一台打开的笔记本电脑，它会让我发疯，而且我的工作会变得更糟。
transcript.english: And worse yet, if I see a open laptop somewhere back there or up here, it drives me nuts, and I do a worse job.

transcript.chinese: 这样就确保了所有正在关注的朋友都不会得到他们想要的性能。
transcript.english: And so that ensures that all of your friends who are paying attention don't get the performance that they came to have.

transcript.chinese: 这就是序言。
transcript.english: So that's it for preamble.

transcript.chinese: 让我们开始吧。
transcript.english: Let's get started.

transcript.chinese: 当然，我们首先要讨论的是如何开始。
transcript.english: First thing we talk about, of course, is how to start.

transcript.chinese: 有些人认为正确的做法是以笑话开始谈话。
transcript.english: Some people think the right thing to do is to start a talk with a joke.

transcript.chinese: 我不推荐这样做，原因是，在演讲开始时，人们仍然把笔记本电脑收起来。
transcript.english: I don't recommend it, and the reason is that, in the beginning of a talk, people are still putting their laptops away.

transcript.chinese: 他们正在适应你的说话参数、你的声音参数，但他们还没有准备好开玩笑。
transcript.english: They're becoming adjusted to your speaking parameters, to your vocal parameters, and they're not ready for a joke.

transcript.chinese: 所以效果不太好。
transcript.english: So it doesn't work very well.

transcript.chinese: 他们通常会失败。
transcript.english: They usually fall flat.

transcript.chinese: 相反，你想做的是从授权承诺开始。
transcript.english: What you want to do instead is start with empowerment promise.

transcript.chinese: 你想告诉人们他们在这一小时结束时会知道哪些他们在一小时开始时不知道的信息。
transcript.english: You want to tell people what they're going to know at the end of the hour that they didn't know at the beginning of the hour.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个赋权的承诺。
transcript.english: It's an empowerment promise.

transcript.chinese: 这就是来这里的原因。
transcript.english: It's the reason for being here.

transcript.chinese: 举个什么例子？
transcript.english: What would be an example?

transcript.chinese: 我懂了。
transcript.english: Oh, I see.

transcript.chinese: 在这 60 分钟结束时，您将了解一些您现在不知道的演讲知识，而您所知道的其中一些知识将对您的生活产生影响。
transcript.english: At the end of this 60 minutes, you will know things about speaking you don't know now, and something among those things you know will make a difference in your life.

transcript.chinese: 是的，这是一个授权承诺，所以这是最好的开始方式。
transcript.english: Yeah, that's an empowerment promise, so that's the best way to start.

transcript.chinese: 现在我已经谈论了一些如何开始，我想做的是给你们一些启发式的例子，当我演讲时，这些例子总是在我的脑海中，
transcript.english: So now that I've talked a little bit about how to start, what I want to do is give you some samples of heuristics that are always on my mind when I give a talk,

transcript.chinese: 第一个启发是，循环讨论这个主题是个好主意。
transcript.english: and first of these heuristics is that it's a good idea to cycle on the subject.

transcript.chinese: 绕过它。
transcript.english: Go around it.

transcript.chinese: 再绕一圈。
transcript.english: Go round it again.

transcript.chinese: 再绕一圈。
transcript.english: Go round it again.

transcript.chinese: 有人说，告诉他你想告诉他什么。
transcript.english: Some people say, tell him what you want to tell him.

transcript.chinese: 告诉他一遍，然后告诉他第三遍，好像人都不聪明一样。
transcript.english: Tell him again, and then tell him a third time, as if people weren't intelligent.

transcript.chinese: 但关键是——原因是——嗯，原因有很多，其中之一是，在任何特定时刻，无论讲座是什么，大约 20% 的人都会感到困惑。
transcript.english: But the point is-- the reason is-- well, there are many reasons, one of which is, at any given moment, about 20% of you will be fogged out no matter what the lecture is.

transcript.chinese: 所以如果你想确保每个人都得到它的概率很高，你需要说三遍。
transcript.english: So if you want to ensure that the probability that everybody gets it is high, you need to say it three times.

transcript.chinese: 所以骑自行车是我一直做的事情之一
transcript.english: So cycling is one of the things that I always

transcript.chinese: 想想我演讲的时候。
transcript.english: think about when I give a talk.

transcript.chinese: 我想到的另一件事是，在解释我的想法时，我想在它周围建立一个围栏，这样它就不会与其他人的想法混淆。
transcript.english: Another thing I think about is, in explaining my idea, I want to build a fence around it so that it's not confused with somebody else's idea.

transcript.chinese: 所以，如果你来自火星，而我正在教你什么是拱门，我可能会对你说，好吧，那就是拱门。
transcript.english: So if you were from Mars, and I was teaching you about what an arch is, I might say to you, well, that's an arch.

transcript.chinese: 不要将其与其他一些东西混淆
transcript.english: And that's not to be confused with some other things

transcript.chinese: 其他人可能会认为——这不是拱门。
transcript.english: that other people might think is-- this is not an arch.

transcript.chinese: 那不是拱门。
transcript.english: That's not an arch.

transcript.chinese: 我正在围绕我的想法筑起一道栅栏，以便将它与其他人的想法区分开来。
transcript.english: I'm building a fence around my idea so that it can be distinguished from somebody else's idea.

transcript.chinese: 因此，从更技术的角度来看，我可能会说，好吧，我的算法可能类似于 - 可能看起来与琼斯的算法相似，除了他的算法是指数算法，而我的算法是线性算法。
transcript.english: So in a more technical sense, I might say, well, my algorithm might similar-- might seem similar to Jones's algorithm, except his is exponential, and mine's linear.

transcript.chinese: 这就是在你的想法周围设置围栏，这样人们就不会混淆它与其他事物的关系。
transcript.english: That's putting a fence around your idea so that people can not be confused about how it might relate to something else.

transcript.chinese: 样本列表中的第三件事
transcript.english: The third thing on this list of samples

transcript.chinese: 是口头标点符号的概念。
transcript.english: is the idea of verbal punctuation.

transcript.chinese: 这里的想法是，因为人们偶尔会迷糊，需要重新上车，所以你需要提供一些标志性的地方，在那里你宣布现在是重新上车的好时机。
transcript.english: And the idea here is that, because people will occasionally fog out and need to get back on the bus, you need to provide some landmark places where you're announcing that it's a good time to get back on.

transcript.chinese: 因此，在这次演讲中，我可能会说一些关于这是我的大纲的事情。
transcript.english: So I might, in this talk, say something about this being my outline.

transcript.chinese: 我们要做的第一件事是讨论如何开始。
transcript.english: The first thing we're going to do is talk about how to start.

transcript.chinese: 然后我们要处理这四个样本，在这四个样本中，我讲了第一个想法，那就是循环。
transcript.english: Then we're going to deal with these four samples, and among these four samples, I've talked about the first idea-- that's cycling.

transcript.chinese: 第二个想法是建筑——现在，第三个想法是口头标点符号。
transcript.english: The second idea, building-- and now, the third idea is verbal punctuation.

transcript.chinese: 所以我正在列举并提供数字。
transcript.english: So I'm enumerating and providing numbers.

transcript.chinese: 我给你一种感觉，谈话中有一个接缝，你可以重新开始。
transcript.english: I'm giving you a sense that there's a seam in the talk, and you can get back on.

transcript.chinese: 所以现在，我们进展顺利，既然我们进展顺利，你能猜出第四个想法可能是什么——一个帮助人们重返公共汽车的想法吗？
transcript.english: So now, we're on a roll, and since we're on a roll, can you guess what fourth idea might be here-- an idea that helps people get back on the bus?

transcript.chinese: 听众：问一个问题。
transcript.english: AUDIENCE: Ask a question.

transcript.chinese: 帕特里克·温斯顿：是吗？
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: Yes?

transcript.chinese: 听众：问一个问题。 [听不清] PATRICK WINSTON： 问个问题，是的。
transcript.english: AUDIENCE: Ask a question. [INAUDIBLE] PATRICK WINSTON: Ask a question, yes.

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Thank you.

transcript.chinese: 所以问一个问题。
transcript.english: So ask a question.

transcript.chinese: 所以我会问一个问题——死空气能有多少？
transcript.english: And so I will ask a question-- how much dead air can there be?

transcript.chinese: 我可以暂停多久？
transcript.english: How long can I pause?

transcript.chinese: 我数了七秒。
transcript.english: I counted seven seconds.

transcript.chinese: 对我来说，七秒钟内不说话似乎是永恒的，但这是等待答案的标准时间。
transcript.english: It seemed like an eternity to me to wait and not say anything for seven seconds, but that's the standard amount of time you can wait for an answer.

transcript.chinese: 当然，必须仔细选择问题。
transcript.english: And of course, the question has to be carefully chosen.

transcript.chinese: 它不能太明显，因为这样人们会不好意思说出来，但答案也不能太难，因为这样就没有人有什么可说的。
transcript.english: It can't be too obvious because then people will be embarrassed to say it, but the answers can't be too hard because then nobody will have anything to say.

transcript.chinese: 因此，这里有一些启发式示例，您可以将其放入您的武器库中，并建立有关演示的想法库。
transcript.english: So here are some sample heuristics you can put in your armamentarium and build up your repertoire of ideas about presentation.

transcript.chinese: 现在，如果这让你相信有一些东西需要知道，有知识，那么我已经成功了，因为我想说服你的是，如果你观察你钦佩并认为是有效的演讲者，并问自己为什么他们成功，那么你就可以建立自己的个人曲目
transcript.english: And now, if this persuades you that there is something to know, that there is knowledge, then I've already succeeded because what I want to convince you of, is if you watch the speakers you admire and feel are effective, and ask yourselves why they're successful, then you can build up your own personal repertoire

transcript.chinese: 并发展自己的个人风格。
transcript.english: and develop your own personal style.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我的基本目标，本次演讲的其余部分是关于我的武器库中的一些我认为有效的东西。
transcript.english: And that's my fundamental objective, and the rest of this talk is about some of the things that are in my armamentarium that I think are effective.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当我们开始讨论这些其他事情时，我们议程上的下一件事是讨论时间和地点。
transcript.english: So next thing on our agenda, as we start to discuss these other things, is a discussion of time and place.

transcript.chinese: 那么你认为什么时候是听讲座的好时机呢？
transcript.english: So what do you think is a good time to have a lecture?

transcript.chinese: 上午 11 点？
transcript.english: 11 AM?

transcript.chinese: 是的。
transcript.english: Yeah.

transcript.chinese: 原因是麻省理工学院的大多数人那时都醒了，几乎没有人再入睡。
transcript.english: And the reason is most people at MIT are awake by then, and hardly anyone has gone back to sleep.

transcript.chinese: 不是刚吃完饭就这样。
transcript.english: It's not right after a meal.

transcript.chinese: 人们不会因为这个或那个而感到疲劳。
transcript.english: People aren't fatigued from this or that.

transcript.chinese: 正是听讲座的好时机。
transcript.english: It's a great time to have a lecture.

transcript.chinese: 那么接下来我要问的问题是这个地方怎么样？
transcript.english: So that brings me, next, to the question of what about the place?

transcript.chinese: 这个地方最重要的是光线充足。
transcript.english: And the most important thing about the place is that it be well lit.

transcript.chinese: 这个房间光线很好。
transcript.english: This room is well lit.

transcript.chinese: 其他类型房间的问题在于，对于我们人类来说，每当灯光熄灭或房间光线昏暗时，就表明我们应该去睡觉了。
transcript.english: Problem with other kinds of rooms is that we humans, whenever the lights go down, or whenever the room is dimly lighted, it signals that we should go to sleep.

transcript.chinese: 所以每当我去某个地方演讲时，即使是今天，我在与视听人员交谈时做的第一件事就是说，让灯开满。
transcript.english: So whenever I go somewhere to give a talk, even today, the first thing I do when I speak to the audio-visual people is say, keep the lights full up.

transcript.chinese: 哦，他们可能会回答，如果我们关掉灯，人们会更好地看到幻灯片，然后我回答说，通过闭上眼皮很难看到幻灯片。
transcript.english: Oh, they might reply, people will see the slides better if we turn the lights off, and then I reply, it's extremely hard to see slides through closed eyelids.

transcript.chinese: 关于这个地方你还有什么要说的吗？
transcript.english: What else can you say about the place?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这个地方应该设防，我的意思是，从口语的意义上来说，就像如果你要抢劫银行，你之前会去银行几次，看看它是什么样子，所以当你抢劫时就不会感到意外。
transcript.english: Well, the place should be cased, and I mean that in a colloquial sense, of like if you're robbing a bank, you would go to the bank some occasions before to see what it's like, so there are no surprises when you do your robbery.

transcript.chinese: 所以每当我去某个地方演讲时，我都会要求主人做的第一件事就是带我去演讲的地方，这样如果有什么奇怪的地方我就能处理。
transcript.english: So whenever I go somewhere to speak, the first thing I ask my host to do is to take me to the place where I'll be speaking so that if there are any weirdnesses, I'll be able to deal with it.

transcript.chinese: 有时，可能需要一些干预。
transcript.english: Sometimes, it might require some intervention.

transcript.chinese: 有时，可能只需要我了解挑战是什么。
transcript.english: Sometimes, it just might require me to understand what the challenges are.

transcript.chinese: 所以当我今天早上来到这里时，我做了我通常做的事情。
transcript.english: So when I came here this morning, I did what I typically do.

transcript.chinese: 我想象所有的座位上都坐满了无私的农场动物，这样我就知道，再糟糕也不会那么糟糕。
transcript.english: I imagined that all the seats were filled with disinterested farm animals, and that way, I knew that, no matter how bad it was, it wouldn't be as bad as that.

transcript.chinese: 所以最后，它应该是合理的——它应该被合理地填充。
transcript.english: So finally, it should be reasonably-- it should be reasonably populated.

transcript.chinese: 情况应该是这样的——如果这个大厅里有 10 个人，每个人都会想知道，发生了什么事情，比没人在这里更有趣。
transcript.english: It should be the case that-- if there were 10 people in this hall, everyone would be wondering, what's going on that's so much more interesting that nobody's here.

transcript.chinese: 所以你想要一个大小合适的地方，不一定要挤满人，但必须有一半以上的人。
transcript.english: So you want to get a right sized place that's-- doesn't have to be packed, but it has to be more than half full.

transcript.chinese: 这些是关于时间和地点的一些想法。
transcript.english: So those are some thoughts about a time and place.

transcript.chinese: 接下来我要谈的是木板、道具和幻灯片的主题。
transcript.english: Next thing I want to talk about is a subject of boards and props and slides.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这些是交易的工具。
transcript.english: Well, these are the tools of the trade.

transcript.chinese: 我相信，当您的目的是提供信息时，这是正确的演讲工具。
transcript.english: I believe that this is the right tool for speaking when your purpose is informing.

transcript.chinese: 当你的目的是揭露时，幻灯片很好，但这是我在通知、教学、演讲时使用的，我使用它有几个原因。
transcript.english: The slides are good when your purpose is exposing, but this is what I use when I'm informing, teaching, lecturing, and there's several reasons why I use it.

transcript.chinese: 一方面，当您使用该板时，您可以获得图形质量。
transcript.english: For one thing, when you use the board, you have a graphic quality.

transcript.chinese: 在这种情况下，当您拥有一块板时，您就可以轻松地利用可以在演示文稿中使用图形的事实。
transcript.english: It's the case that, when you have a board, then you can easily exploit the fact that you can use graphics in your presentation.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我喜欢的图形质量，接下来我喜欢的是速度属性。
transcript.english: So that's the graphic quality that I like, and the next thing I like is the speed property.

transcript.chinese: 你在黑板上写字的速度大约就是人们吸收思想的速度。
transcript.english: The speed with which you write on the blackboard is approximately the speed at which people can absorb ideas.

transcript.chinese: 如果你翻阅一堆幻灯片，没有人能翻得那么快。
transcript.english: If you go flipping through a bunch of slides, nobody can go that fast.

transcript.chinese: 最后，董事会的一大特性是它可以成为目标。
transcript.english: Finally, one great property of a board is that it can be a target.

transcript.chinese: 许多刚开始说话的人发现自己突然意识到自己的手。
transcript.english: Many people who are novices at speaking find themselves suddenly aware of their hands.

transcript.chinese: 就好像他们的手是隐私部位，不应该在公共场合暴露一样，所以他们马上就把手放进口袋里，这在世界上的一些地方被认为是一种侮辱。
transcript.english: It's as if their hands were private parts that shouldn't be exposed in public, so right away, they go into the pockets, and this is considered insulting in some parts of the world.

transcript.chinese: 或者，也许双手会像这样向后伸。
transcript.english: Or alternatively, maybe the hands will go in back like this.

transcript.chinese: 我曾经在塞尔维亚的一座修道院里，我们一进去，我的主人，一位修女就向我们走来，给我们提供了茶点。
transcript.english: I was once in a convent in Serbia, and my host-- as soon as we entered, a nun came up to us and offered us a refreshment.

transcript.chinese: 我正要说，不，谢谢你，他说，要么吃那个东西，要么死。
transcript.english: And I was about to say, no, thank you, and he said, eat that stuff or die.

transcript.chinese: 这是当地风俗和礼貌的问题。
transcript.english: It's a question of local custom and politeness.

transcript.chinese: 但在那儿发生任何事情之前，修女像这样把我的手拉出来，因为在那种文化中，把手放在背后是非常侮辱的。
transcript.english: But then before anything happened there, the nun pulled my hands out like this because it was extraordinarily insulting in that culture to have your hands behind your back.

transcript.chinese: 那么这是为什么呢？
transcript.english: So why is that?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，通常认为这与你是否隐藏武器有关。
transcript.english: Well, it's usually supposed that that's that it has to do with whether you're concealing a weapon.

transcript.chinese: 因此，如果你的手插在口袋里或背后，那么看起来你可能拥有武器，这就是我所说的美德的意思——棋盘的这些美德之一。
transcript.english: So if your hands are in your pockets or behind your back, then it looks like you might have a weapon, and that's what I mean by the virtue-- one of these virtues of the board.

transcript.chinese: 现在，你的手有事可做了。
transcript.english: Now, you have something to do with your hands.

transcript.chinese: 你可以指出这些东西。
transcript.english: You can point out the stuff.

transcript.chinese: 我有一次观看 Seymour Papert 的演讲，我觉得非常棒。
transcript.english: I was once watching Seymour Papert give a lecture, and I thought it was terrific.

transcript.chinese: 于是我又去了第二次——第一次是吸收内容，第二次是注意风格。
transcript.english: So I went a second time-- first time to absorb the content, second time to note style.

transcript.chinese: 我发现帕普特一直指着黑板。
transcript.english: And what I discovered is that Papert was constantly pointing at the board.

transcript.chinese: 然后我想了一会儿，我注意到他所指的东西与他所说的没有任何关系。
transcript.english: And then I thought about it a little while, and I noted that none of the stuff he was pointing to had anything to do with what he was saying.

transcript.chinese: 尽管如此，这是一种有效的技术。
transcript.english: Nevertheless, it was an effective technique.

transcript.chinese: 这只是关于黑板优点的一点介绍。
transcript.english: So that's just a little bit about the virtue of blackboards.

transcript.chinese: 现在，我想谈谈道具。
transcript.english: Now, I want to talk about props.

transcript.chinese: 道具知识的保管者是剧作家。
transcript.english: The custodians of knowledge about props are the playwrights.

transcript.chinese: 几十年前，我看过易卜生的一部戏剧。
transcript.english: Many decades ago, I saw a play by Henrik Ibsen.

transcript.chinese: 是海达·加布勒。
transcript.english: It was Hedda Gabler.

transcript.chinese: 我依稀记得这是关于一个婚姻不幸福的女人的故事，她的丈夫正在与其他人竞争一份学术工作。
transcript.english: I remember vaguely that it was about a woman in an unhappy marriage, and her husband was in competition for an academic job with somebody else.

transcript.chinese: 他之所以会输，部分原因是他很无聊，部分原因是竞争对手刚刚写了一本精彩的书。
transcript.english: And he was going to lose partly because he was boring, and partly because the competitor had just written a magnificent book.

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一句，这是在复印机和计算机出现之前的时代。
transcript.english: By the way, this is back in the days before there were copying machines and computers.

transcript.chinese: 无论如何，当戏剧开始时，有一个大腹便便的炉子，而在戏剧开始时，大腹便便的炉子，门开着，只有一些微微发光的余烬。
transcript.english: Anyhow, as the play opens, there's a potbellied stove, and in the beginning of the play, the potbelly stove, with its open door, just has some slightly glowing embers.

transcript.chinese: 但大腹便便的炉子总是在那里，随着戏剧中的紧张气氛加剧，你看到这份手稿，易卜生如此巧妙地使用的道具，你就知道
transcript.english: But the potbellied stove is always there, and as tension mounts in the play, and you see this manuscript, this prop that Ibsen so artfully used, you just know

transcript.chinese: 某件事将会发生，因为随着戏剧的继续，火变得越来越大，越来越热，最后全部被吞噬，而你只知道那份手稿将进入那场大火。
transcript.english: that something's going to happen, because as the play goes on, the fire gets bigger and hotter and finally all consuming, and you just know that that manuscript is going to go into that fire.

transcript.chinese: 这件难忘的事情就是我对这部剧的记忆。
transcript.english: This memorable thing is what I remember about the play.

transcript.chinese: 所以剧作家已经把这一切弄清楚了，但另一方面，他们并不是唯一可以使用道具的人。
transcript.english: So playwrights have got this all figured out, but on the other hand, they're not the only people who can use props.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个使用道具的例子，也是由 Seymour Papert 提供的。
transcript.english: Here's an example of the use of a prop, also due to Seymour Papert.

transcript.chinese: 他正在谈论以正确的方式看待问题的重要性，这是
transcript.english: He was talking about how it's important to look at the problem in the right way, and here's

transcript.chinese: 这个例子不仅可以教会你这一点，而且还可以让你在机械工程领域的朋友感到尴尬。
transcript.english: an example that not only teaches that, but makes it possible for you to embarrass your friends in mechanical engineering.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就是你要做的。
transcript.english: So here's what you do.

transcript.chinese: 拿一个自行车轮，你就开始让它旋转。
transcript.english: Take a bicycle wheel, and you start it spinning.

transcript.chinese: 然后你在轴上施加一些扭矩，或者等效地，你吹边缘。
transcript.english: And then you put some torque on the axle, or equivalently, you blow on the edge.

transcript.chinese: 问题是，事情是这样发展的，还是那样发展的？
transcript.english: And the issue is, does it go that way, or does it go that way?

transcript.chinese: 现在，机械工程师会立即说，哦，是的，我明白了——右手螺旋轮。
transcript.english: Now, the mechanical engineers will immediately say, oh, yes, I see-- right hand screw wheel.

transcript.chinese: 他们会将手指放在这个位置，但却忘记了如何将手指与问题的各个方面保持一致。
transcript.english: And they'll put their fingers in this position, but forget exactly how to align their fingers with the various aspects of the problem.

transcript.chinese: 因此，通常情况下，他们做对的概率约为 50%。
transcript.english: And so it's usually the case that they get it right with about a 50% probability.

transcript.chinese: 所以他们接受的非常好的教育让他们达到了相当于掷硬币的地步。
transcript.english: So their very fancy education gets them up to the point where they're equivalent to flipping a coin.

transcript.chinese: 但不一定非要这样
transcript.english: But it doesn't have to be that way

transcript.chinese: 因为你可以稍微不同地思考这个问题。
transcript.english: because you can think about the problem a little differently.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就是你要做的。
transcript.english: So here's what you do.

transcript.chinese: 你拿一些胶带，像这样把它放在车轮的周围。
transcript.english: You take some duct tape, and you put it around the part of the wheel like that.

transcript.chinese: 现在，您开始考虑的不是整个车轮，而是管道胶带下面的一小部分。
transcript.english: And now, you start to think about, not the whole wheel, but just a little piece that's underneath the duct tape.

transcript.chinese: 所以在这里，那块东西滚过顶部，此时，你用一股空气吹它。
transcript.english: So here, that piece comes rolling over the top, and at this point, you blow on it with a puff of air.

transcript.chinese: 忘记车轮的其余部分，胶带下面的那个小部件会发生什么？
transcript.english: Forgetting about the rest of the wheel, what happens to that little piece that's under the duct tape?

transcript.chinese: 它一定想朝那个方向走，因为你那样撞击它。
transcript.english: It must want to go that way because you banged on it like that.

transcript.chinese: 已经这样下去了。
transcript.english: It's already going down like that.

transcript.chinese: 那么下一块呢？
transcript.english: And what about the next piece?

transcript.chinese: 同样的事情。
transcript.english: Same thing.

transcript.chinese: 下一块？
transcript.english: Next piece?

transcript.chinese: 同样的事情。
transcript.english: Same thing.

transcript.chinese: 所以唯一可能发生的事情就是车轮像那样转动。
transcript.english: So the only thing that can happen is that the wheel goes over like that.

transcript.chinese: 所以现在，你再也不会想知道了，因为你正在以正确的方式思考问题，并且它通过道具的使用得到了证明。
transcript.english: And so now, you'll never wonder again because you're thinking about the problem in the right way, and it's demonstrated by the use of a prop.

transcript.chinese: 完成后您可以尝试一下。
transcript.english: You can try this after we're done.

transcript.chinese: 我喜欢记住的另一个例子是我参加 8.01 课程时的一个例子。
transcript.english: Another example I like to remember is one from when I was taking 8.01.

transcript.chinese: 艾伦·拉扎勒斯（Alan Lazarus）是当时的讲师，他讲的是能量守恒、动能守恒和势能守恒。
transcript.english: Alan Lazarus was the instructor at the time, and he was talking about the conservation of energy, kinetic and potential.

transcript.chinese: 天花板上有一根 26-100 的长电线，连接着一个更大的钢球，但只有一根——不是像这样的。
transcript.english: And there was a long wire in a ceiling in 26-100 attached to a much bigger steel ball, but one-- not one like this.

transcript.chinese: 拉撒路就这样把球靠在墙上。
transcript.english: And Lazarus took the ball up against the wall like this.

transcript.chinese: 他把头平靠在墙上稳定自己，然后松开手，钟摆花了好几秒钟才来回转动，然后轻轻地吻了拉扎勒斯的鼻子。
transcript.english: He put his head flat against the wall to steady himself, and then he let go, and the pendulum takes many seconds to go over and back, and then gently kisses Lazarus's nose.

transcript.chinese: 所以你有很多时间思考，这个人真的相信能量守恒。
transcript.english: And so you have many seconds to think, this guy really believes in the conservation of energy.

transcript.chinese: 不要在家尝试这个。
transcript.english: Do not try this at home.

transcript.chinese: 问题是，第一次这样做时，你可能不会放手。
transcript.english: The problem is that, the first time you do this, you may not just let go.

transcript.chinese: 人类有一种自然的推动倾向。
transcript.english: There's a natural human tendency to push.

transcript.chinese: 这就是关于道具的一些内容。
transcript.english: So that's a little bit on a subject of props.

transcript.chinese: 这很有趣。
transcript.english: It's interesting.

transcript.chinese: 每当进行调查时，学生总是说多用粉笔，少用 PowerPoint。
transcript.english: Whenever surveys are taken, students always say more chalk, less PowerPoint.

transcript.chinese: 为什么会这样呢？
transcript.english: And why would that be?

transcript.chinese: 道具也非常有效。
transcript.english: Props are also very effective.

transcript.chinese: 为什么会这样呢？
transcript.english: Why would that be?

transcript.chinese: 我将告诉你我对此的疯狂边缘观点。
transcript.english: I'll give you my lunatic fringe view on this.

transcript.chinese: 这与我所说的同理心镜像有关。
transcript.english: It has to do with what I would call empathetic mirroring.

transcript.chinese: 当你坐在那里看着我在黑板上写字时，我相信你大脑中所有那些小镜像神经元都会被激活，你可以感觉到自己在黑板上写字。
transcript.english: When you're sitting up there watching me write on the board, all those little mirror neurons in your head, I believe, become actuated, and you can feel yourself writing on the blackboard.

transcript.chinese: 更重要的是，当我谈论这个钢球向那边和这边移动时，你可以感觉到球，就好像你是我一样，而你不能用幻灯片做到这一点。
transcript.english: And even more so, when I talk about this steel ball going that way and this way, you can feel the ball as if you were me, and you can't do that with a slide.

transcript.chinese: 你不能用一张图片来做到这一点。
transcript.english: You can't do it with a picture.

transcript.chinese: 你需要在物质世界中看到它。
transcript.english: You need to see it in the physical world.

transcript.chinese: 这就是为什么我认为——哦，是的，当然是——也涉及到速度问题，必须将其分开。
transcript.english: That's why I think that-- oh, yes, of course, it's-- there are speed questions involved, too, that have to be separated out.

transcript.chinese: 但我认为同理心镜像就是道具和黑板的使用如此有效的原因。
transcript.english: But I think the empathetic mirroring is why props and the use of a blackboard are so effective.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，让我们看看——哦，是的，还有一件事是关于工具的，这与幻灯片的使用有关。
transcript.english: Well, let's see-- oh, yes, there is one more thing by way of the tools, and that has to do with the use of slides.

transcript.chinese: 我再说一遍，我认为它们是为了揭示想法，而不是为了教授想法，但这就是我们在工作演讲或会议演讲中所做的——揭示想法。
transcript.english: I repeat, I think they're for exposing ideas, not for teaching ideas, but that's what we do in a job talk or conference talk-- expose ideas.

transcript.chinese: 我们不教他们。
transcript.english: We don't teach them.

transcript.chinese: 那么让我告诉你一些我对此的看法。
transcript.english: So let me tell you a little bit about my views on that.

transcript.chinese: 我记得有一次，我在洛根机场 A 航站楼。
transcript.english: I remember, once, I was in Terminal A at Logan Airport.

transcript.chinese: 我刚刚从一个非常痛苦的会议回来，这次飞行真的很糟糕。
transcript.english: I'd just come back from a really miserable conference, and the flight was really horrible.

transcript.chinese: 它是那种感觉就像一台不平衡的洗衣机的机器之一。
transcript.english: It was one of those that feels like an unbalanced washing machine.

transcript.chinese: 这是我一生中唯一一次，我决定在上车的路上停下来喝杯咖啡，放松一下。
transcript.english: And for the only time in my life, I decided to stop on my way to my car and have a cup of coffee and relax a little bit.

transcript.chinese: 当我在那里呆了几分钟时，有人走过来对我说，你是温斯顿教授吗？
transcript.english: And as I was there for a few minutes, someone came up to me and said, are you Professor Winston?

transcript.chinese: 我想是的，我说。
transcript.english: I think so, I said.

transcript.chinese: 我不知道。
transcript.english: I don't know.

transcript.chinese: 我想我是想搞笑。
transcript.english: I guess I was trying to be funny.

transcript.chinese: 无论如何，他说，我正在前往欧洲进行工作演讲的路上。
transcript.english: In any event, he said, I'm on my way to Europe to give a job talk.

transcript.chinese: 你会批评我的幻灯片吗？
transcript.english: Would you mine critiquing my slides?

transcript.chinese: 一点也不，我说。
transcript.english: Not at all, I said.

transcript.chinese: 你有太多，他们有太多的话。
transcript.english: You have too many, and they have too many words.

transcript.chinese: 你怎么知道，他说，心想也许我以前看过他的演讲。
transcript.english: How did you know, he said, thinking perhaps I had seen a talk of his before.

transcript.chinese: 我没有。
transcript.english: I hadn't.

transcript.chinese: 我的回答是，因为它总是正确的。
transcript.english: My reply was, because it's always true.

transcript.chinese: 总是有太多的幻灯片，总是有太多的文字。
transcript.english: There are always too many slides, always too many words.

transcript.chinese: 让我向您展示一些如何不使用幻灯片的极端例子。
transcript.english: So let me show you some extreme examples of how not to use slides.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，对于这个演示，我需要远到这里，当我到达这里时，我可以开始说这样的话，你不应该做的事情之一就是阅读你的透明胶片。
transcript.english: Well, for this demonstration, I need to be way over here and when I get over here, then I can start to say things like, one of the things you shouldn't do is read your transparencies.

transcript.chinese: 你的听众中的人知道如何阅读，而阅读只会让他们烦恼。
transcript.english: People in your audience know how to read, and reading will just annoy them.

transcript.chinese: 另外，您应该确保每张幻灯片上只有几个单词，并且这些单词易于阅读。
transcript.english: Also, you should be sure that you have only a few words on each transparency, and that the words are easy to read.

transcript.chinese: 我希望我让你发疯，因为我犯下了各种各样的罪行，首先是幻灯片上的文字太多了。
transcript.english: And I hope I'm driving you crazy because I'm committing all kinds of crimes, the first of which is that there are too many words on the slide.

transcript.chinese: 第二个是，我在那边，幻灯片也在那边。
transcript.english: Second of which is, I'm way over there, and the slide's way over there.

transcript.chinese: 因此，您会感受到在幻灯片和扬声器之间来回切换的网球比赛感觉。
transcript.english: So you get into this tennis match feeling of shifting back and forth between the slide and the speaker.

transcript.chinese: 您希望幻灯片成为什么
transcript.english: You want the slides to be condiments to what

transcript.chinese: 的调味品你是说，不是主要事件，或者相反。
transcript.english: you're saying, not the main event or the opposite way around.

transcript.chinese: 那么我们该如何解决这个问题呢？
transcript.english: So how can we fix this?

transcript.chinese: 第一步是清除背景垃圾。
transcript.english: Step number one is to get rid of the background junk.

transcript.chinese: 这总是让人分心。
transcript.english: That's always distraction.

transcript.chinese: 第二步是去掉这些词。
transcript.english: Step number two is to get rid of the words.

transcript.chinese: 当我将文字减少到这些时，那么我以前读过的所有内容都无权说，因为它不在幻灯片上。
transcript.english: When I reduced the words to these, then everything I read a previous time, I'm not licensed to say, because it's not on the slide.

transcript.chinese: 我不再阅读我的幻灯片，但我说的是上一个示例中幻灯片上写的内容。
transcript.english: I'm not reading my slides anymore, but I'm saying what was written on the slides in a previous example.

transcript.chinese: 那么我们还能做些什么来简化这个过程呢？
transcript.english: So what else can we do to simplify this?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我们可以去掉标志。
transcript.english: Well, we can get rid of the logos.

transcript.chinese: 我们不需要它们。
transcript.english: We don't need them.

transcript.chinese: 简化。
transcript.english: Simplification.

transcript.chinese: 我们还能做什么？
transcript.english: What else can we do?

transcript.chinese: 摆脱标题。
transcript.english: Get rid of the title.

transcript.chinese: 现在，我想和大家谈谈幻灯片准备的一些规则。
transcript.english: Now, I want to talk to you about some rules for slide preparation.

transcript.chinese: 我告诉你标题。
transcript.english: I'm telling you the title.

transcript.chinese: 它不必在上面。
transcript.english: It doesn't have to be up there.

transcript.chinese: 通过减少幻灯片上的字数，我可以让您更多地关注我，而不是幻灯片上写的内容。
transcript.english: By reducing the number of words on the slide, I'm allowing you to pay more attention to me and less to what's written on the slide.

transcript.chinese: 我之前提到过——我们只有一种语言处理器，我们可以用它来阅读内容或听演讲者讲话。
transcript.english: I mentioned it before-- we-- have only one language processor, and we can either use it to read stuff or to listen to the speaker.

transcript.chinese: 因此，如果幻灯片上的文字太多，就会迫使观众阅读这些内容而不是倾听。
transcript.english: And so if we have too many words on the slide, it forces people in the audience to read this stuff and not listen.

transcript.chinese: 几年前，我的一个学生做了一个实验。
transcript.english: A student of mine did an experiment a few years ago.

transcript.chinese: 他教给一些学生一些基于网络的编程思想。
transcript.english: He taught some students some web-based programming ideas.

transcript.chinese: 他说，一半的信息在幻灯片上，另一半，然后对于对照组，他颠倒了它。
transcript.english: Half the information was on slides, he said the other half, and then for a control group, he reversed it.

transcript.chinese: 问题是，受试者（也就是说，他兄弟会的新生）最记得什么，他说了什么，或者他们在幻灯片上读到了什么？
transcript.english: And the question was, what did the subjects-- that is to say, freshmen at his fraternity-- what did the subjects remember best, what he said, or what they read on the slide?

transcript.chinese: 答案是他们在幻灯片上读到的内容。
transcript.english: And the answer is, what they read on the slide.

transcript.chinese: 当他们的幻灯片上有很多材料时，他们不会注意演讲者。
transcript.english: When their slides have a lot of material on it, they don't pay attention to the speaker.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，在行动后报告中，其中一名受试者说，我希望你没有说那么多。
transcript.english: In fact, in the after action report, one of the subjects said, I wish you hadn't talked so much.

transcript.chinese: 这让人分心。
transcript.english: It was distracting.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，最后一项是消除混乱。
transcript.english: Well, the last item is eliminate clutter.

transcript.chinese: 这里有些混乱。
transcript.english: Here's some clutter.

transcript.chinese: 即使是那些子弹也没有理由。
transcript.english: No reason even for those bullets.

transcript.chinese: 因此，字数过多的问题是微软允许你使用太小的字体所犯下的罪行的后果。
transcript.english: So the too many words problem is a consequence of a crime Microsoft has committed by allowing you to use fonts that are too small.

transcript.chinese: 因此，你们都应该有一个像这样的示例幻灯片，您可以使用它来确定易于阅读的最小字体大小。 [听不清]，您觉得这些怎么样？
transcript.english: So you should all have a sample slide like this that you can use to determine what the minimum font size is that's easily legible. [INAUDIBLE],, what do you think of those?

transcript.chinese: 观众：哪个尺寸合适？
transcript.english: AUDIENCE: Which size is right?

transcript.chinese: 帕特里克·温斯顿： 那是什么？
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: What's that?

transcript.chinese: 听众：你问我多大合适？
transcript.english: AUDIENCE: Did you ask me what size is right?

transcript.chinese: PATRICK WINSTON： 是的，至少，也许吧。
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: Yeah, minimum, maybe.
AUDIENCE: 40 or 50.

transcript.chinese: PATRICK WINSTON：是的，他说 40 或 50。
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: Yeah, he says 40 or 50.

transcript.chinese: 我认为这是正确的。
transcript.english: I think that's about right.

transcript.chinese: 35 开始变得太小，不一定是因为您无法阅读它，而是因为您可能使用它在幻灯片上显示太多单词。
transcript.english: 35 is beginning to get too small, not necessarily because you can't read it, but because you're probably using it to get too many words on the slide.

transcript.chinese: 我们还有哪些犯罪行为？
transcript.english: What other crimes do we have?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我们有激光笔犯罪。
transcript.english: Well, we have the laser pointer crime.

transcript.chinese: 为此，在过去，当我们没有激光笔时，我们使用木制笔，人们会四处挥舞这些东西。
transcript.english: And for that-- in the old days, when we didn't have laser pointers, we used wooden ones, and people would go waving these things around.

transcript.chinese: 很快，它几乎就像一场接力棒比赛，所以这就是我过去推荐的处理这种指针的方法。
transcript.english: And pretty soon it became almost like a baton twirling contest, so here's what I recommended in the old days for dealing with this kind of pointer.

transcript.chinese: 这是使用道具的示例。
transcript.english: This is an example of use of a prop.

transcript.chinese: 吉姆·格拉斯 (Jim Glass) 大约 20 年前看到了这个演讲，他说，哦，是的，我记得那个演讲。
transcript.english: Jim Glass up there saw this talk about 20 years ago, and said, oh, yeah, I remember that talk.

transcript.chinese: 这就是你破坏指针的地方。
transcript.english: That's the one where you broke the pointer.

transcript.chinese: 令人惊奇的是，道具往往是被记住的东西。
transcript.english: It's amazing how props tend to be the things that are remembered.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，现在我们不再有物理指针了。
transcript.english: Well, now, we don't have physical pointers anymore.

transcript.chinese: 我们有激光笔。
transcript.english: We've got laser pointers.

transcript.chinese: 没有更多的人因为这类事情而陷入癫痫发作，这真是一个奇迹。
transcript.english: It's a wonder more people aren't driven into epileptic fits over this sort of stuff.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这就是经常发生的情况。
transcript.english: Well, here's what tends to happen.

transcript.chinese: 看看那个。
transcript.english: Look at that.

transcript.chinese: 这是一幅可爱的递归图片，通过将激光束放在我的后脑勺上，我可以成为它的一部分。
transcript.english: It's a lovely recursive picture, and I can become part of it by putting that laser beam right on the back of my head up there.

transcript.chinese: 然后你看到了什么？
transcript.english: Then what do you see?

transcript.chinese: 你看到我的后脑勺了。
transcript.english: You see the back of my head.

transcript.chinese: 我没有眼神交流，没有参与，什么都没有。
transcript.english: I have no eye contact, no engagement, nothing.

transcript.chinese: 有一天，我和一位学生坐在一起看一场演讲，她说，你知道吗，我们都可以离开，而他不会知道。
transcript.english: I was sitting with a student watching a talk one day, and she said, you know what, we could all leave, and he wouldn't know.

transcript.chinese: 那么当您使用激光笔时会发生什么呢？
transcript.english: So what happens when you use a laser pointer?

transcript.chinese: 如果不转动头部并将其指向某物，就无法使用激光笔，而当您这样做时，您就会与观众失去联系。
transcript.english: You can't use a laser pointer without turning your head and pointing it at something, and when you do that, you lose contact with the audience.

transcript.chinese: 你不想这样做。
transcript.english: You don't want to do it.

transcript.chinese: 那么，如果您需要识别图像中的某些内容，并且不想用激光指向它，该怎么办？
transcript.english: So what do you do if you need to identify something in your image, and you don't want to point at it with a laser?

transcript.chinese: 这就是你要做的。
transcript.english: This is what you do.

transcript.chinese: 在那里放一个小箭头并说，
transcript.english: Put a little arrow on there and say,

transcript.chinese: 现在，看看第一个箭头末端的那个人。
transcript.english: now, look at that guy at the end of arrow number one.

transcript.chinese: 您不需要激光笔来做到这一点。
transcript.english: You don't need to have laser pointer to do that.

transcript.chinese: 罪过太重了——当人们要求我审阅演示文稿时，我要求他们将其打印出来并放在桌子上。
transcript.english: The too-heavy crime-- when people ask me to review a presentation, I ask them to print it out and lay it out on a table.

transcript.chinese: 当他们这样做时，很容易看出谈话是否太沉重、文字太多、空气不够、空白不够、图像不够。
transcript.english: When they do that, it's easy to see whether the talk is too heavy, too much text, not enough air, not enough white space, not enough imagery.

transcript.chinese: 这是此类谈话的一个很好的例子——太沉重了。
transcript.english: This is a good example of such a talk-- way too heavy.

transcript.chinese: 演示者利用小字体在幻灯片上展示了他想要的内容。
transcript.english: The presenter has taken advantage of a small font sizes to get as much on the slide as he wanted.

transcript.chinese: 这里还有很多其他罪行，但事实太严重了
transcript.english: Lots of other crimes here, but the too-heavy-- the fact

transcript.chinese: 我想说明的是它太重了。
transcript.english: that it's too heavy is what I wanted to illustrate.

transcript.chinese: 相比之下，这是我几年前发表的另一场演讲。
transcript.english: So here, by contrast, another talk-- one I gave a few years ago.

transcript.chinese: 这不是——这不是一个深入的技术演讲，但我向你们展示它，因为里面有空气。
transcript.english: It's not-- it wasn't a deeply technical talk, but I show it to you because there's air in it.

transcript.chinese: 大部分都是事物的图片。
transcript.english: It's mostly pictures of things.

transcript.chinese: 有三到四张幻灯片上有文字，但当我看到这些时，我会给观众时间阅读它们。
transcript.english: There are three or four slides that have text on them, but when I come to those, I give the audience time to read them.

transcript.chinese: 它们之所以存在是因为它们可能具有一定的历史意义。
transcript.english: And they're there because they might have some historical significance.

transcript.chinese: 第一张包含大量文本的幻灯片是 1957 年达特茅斯人工智能会议提案的摘录。
transcript.english: The first slide with a lot of text on it is an extraction from the 1957-- from the proposal for the 1957 AI conference at Dartmouth.

transcript.chinese: 非常有趣的事件，
transcript.english: Extraordinarily interesting event,

transcript.chinese: 从提案中提取历史有助于加深这一点。
transcript.english: and that historical extraction from the proposal helps drive that point home.

transcript.chinese: 我们这里还有什么？
transcript.english: What else have we got here?

transcript.chinese: 哦，是的，你今天的词汇。
transcript.english: Oh, yeah, your vocabulary word for the day.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个hapax Legomenon。
transcript.english: This is an hapax legomenon.

transcript.chinese: 这意味着，这是一种您可以在演示文稿中只使用一次的幻灯片。
transcript.english: What that means is, this is the kind of slide you can get away with exactly once in your presentation.

transcript.chinese: 这张幻灯片在几年前就受到了一些关注，因为它通过展示阿富汗治理的复杂性是多么的不可思议。
transcript.english: This is a slide that got some currency some years ago because it shows the complexity of governing in Afghanistan by showing how impossibly complex it is.

transcript.chinese: 这是观众无法理解的，这就是重点，但你不能有很多这样的东西。
transcript.english: It's something you in the audience can't understand, and that's the point, but you can't have many of these.

transcript.chinese: 您可以为每件作品准备一份，为每份演示准备一份，为每篇论文准备一份，为每本书准备一份。
transcript.english: You can have one per work, one per presentation, one per paper, one per book.

transcript.chinese: 这就是 haax Legomenon，这就是一个例子。
transcript.english: That's what hapax legomenon is, and this is an example of it.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我已经向你展示了一些犯罪行为。
transcript.english: Well, I've shown you some crimes.

transcript.chinese: 那么您可能会问，这些犯罪行为真的发生过吗？
transcript.english: So you might be asking, do these crimes actually occur?

transcript.chinese: 他们就是这么做的。 [笑声] 手插口袋犯罪。
transcript.english: So they do. [LAUGHTER] There's the hands in the pockets crime.

transcript.chinese: 这里有犯罪以及时间和地点的选择。
transcript.english: There's a crime and time and place selection here.

transcript.chinese: 这就是您到达巴托斯剧院的方式。
transcript.english: This is how you get to the Bartos Theater.

transcript.chinese: 你要做的第一件事就是走上媒体实验室的这些台阶，然后穿过这个大的开放空间，然后右转沿着这条走廊。 [笑声] 此时，每当我进去时，我都会想拐角处是否有刑具。
transcript.english: First thing you do is you get on these steps over at the Media Lab, then you cross this large open space, then you turn right down this corridor. [LAUGHTER] At this point, whenever I go in there, I wonder if there are torture implements around the corner.

transcript.chinese: [笑声] 当你进入那里时，你就进入了这个黑暗、阴暗的地方。
transcript.english: [LAUGHTER] And then when you get in there, you get into this dark, gloomy place.

transcript.chinese: 所以当他们称之为巴托斯剧院时，它的名字很好，因为它是一个你可以看电影的地方，但它不是一个你可以演讲的地方。
transcript.english: So it's well named when they call it the Bartos Theater because it's a place where you can watch a movie, but it's not a place where you can give a talk.

transcript.chinese: 现在，关于它是否会发生这一主题，这是我不久前在 Stata 参加的一次演讲。
transcript.english: Now, on a subject of does it happen, here's a talk I attended a while back in Stata.

transcript.chinese: 请注意，扬声器距离幻灯片较远。
transcript.english: Notice that the speaker is far away from the slides.

transcript.chinese: 扬声器使用激光笔。
transcript.english: Speaker's using a laser pointer.

transcript.chinese: 你对我说，这里发生了什么事？
transcript.english: And you say to me, well, what's happening here?

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一下，这是80 - 80 号！演示文稿的幻灯片。
transcript.english: It's, by the way, the 80th - 80th! slide of the presentation.

transcript.chinese: 请注意，它扩展了以下文字：这是 10 张结论幻灯片中的第一张。 [笑声] 那么观众的反应是什么？
transcript.english: Notice that it extends with the words, this is the first of 10 conclusions slides. [LAUGHTER] So what's the audience reaction?

transcript.chinese: 那是会议的赞助商。
transcript.english: That's the sponsor of the meeting.

transcript.chinese: [笑声]他正在阅读他的电子邮件。
transcript.english: [LAUGHTER] He's reading his email.

transcript.chinese: 这是会议的共同发起人。
transcript.english: This is the co-sponsor of the meeting.

transcript.chinese: 他正在检查午餐菜单。 [笑声] 这个人怎么样？
transcript.english: He's examining the lunch menu. [LAUGHTER] What about this person?

transcript.chinese: 这个人看起来很专注，但只是因为这是一张静态图片。
transcript.english: This person looks like he's paying attention, but just because it's a still picture.

transcript.chinese: 如果您观看视频，您会看到类似这样的内容 [哈欠]。
transcript.english: If you were to see a video, what you would see is something like this [YAWNS].

transcript.chinese: [笑声]所以是的，它确实发生了。
transcript.english: [LAUGHTER] So yeah, it does happen.

transcript.chinese: 好了，现在，这是对工具的快速回顾。
transcript.english: Well, now, that's a quick review of tools.

transcript.chinese: 现在，我想谈谈一些特殊情况。
transcript.english: Now, I want to talk about some special cases.

transcript.chinese: 我们可以稍微讨论一下通知，或者换句话说，做我现在正在做的事情。
transcript.english: We could talk a little bit about the informing or to say another way, doing what I'm doing now.

transcript.chinese: 但我只想对此说几句话。
transcript.english: But I'll just say a few words about that.

transcript.chinese: 在这种演讲中，你想以一个承诺开始，就像我在我们现在正在经历的这一个小时里所做的那样。
transcript.english: In that kind of presentation, you want to start with a promise like I did for this hour that we're going through now.

transcript.chinese: 然后就涉及到如何激励人们的问题？
transcript.english: And then it comes to the question of how do you inspire people?

transcript.chinese: 我已经做过这个演讲很长时间了，几年前，我们系主任说，请你给一位新教师做这个演讲，一定要强调如何激励学生。
transcript.english: I've given this talk for a long time, and a few years ago, our department chairman said, would you please give this talk to a new faculty, and be sure to emphasize what it takes to inspire students.

transcript.chinese: 奇怪的是，我以前没有想过这个问题。
transcript.english: And strangely, I hadn't thought about that question before.

transcript.chinese: 于是我开始了一项调查。
transcript.english: So I started a survey.

transcript.chinese: 我与一些即将入学的新生顾问进行了交谈，也与高级教师以及介于两者之间的所有人员进行了交谈，了解他们是如何受到启发的。
transcript.english: I'd talked to some of my incoming freshmen advisees, and I talked to senior faculty and everything in between about how they've been inspired.

transcript.chinese: 我从新生身上发现，他们受到了一些高中老师的启发，老师告诉他们他们可以做到。
transcript.english: What I found from the incoming freshmen is that they were inspired by some high school teacher who told them they could do it.

transcript.chinese: 我在高级教师中发现，他们受到帮助他们以新方式看待问题的人的启发。
transcript.english: What I found in the senior faculty, they were inspired by someone who helped them see a problem in a new way.

transcript.chinese: 我从每个人身上看到的是，当有人对他们正在做的事情表现出热情时，他们就会受到启发。
transcript.english: And what I saw from everyone is that they were inspired when someone exhibited passion about what they were doing, exhibited passion about what they were doing.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是一种鼓舞人心的方式。
transcript.english: So that's one way to be inspiring.

transcript.chinese: 这对我来说很容易，因为我是做人工智能的。
transcript.english: It's easy for me because I do artificial intelligence.

transcript.chinese: 你怎么能对人工智能不感兴趣呢？ [笑声]我的意思是，如果你对人工智能不感兴趣，你可能对有趣的事情也不感兴趣。
transcript.english: And how can you not be interested in artificial intelligence? [LAUGHTER] I mean, if you're not interested in artificial intelligence, you're probably not interested in interesting things.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当我在人工智能课程上授课时，我很自然地会谈论我认为很酷的东西以及一些新想法是多么令人兴奋。
transcript.english: So when I'm lecturing in my AI class, it's natural for me to talk about what I think is cool and how exciting some new idea is.

transcript.chinese: 因此，这种激情的表达在传达有关鼓舞人心的问题时会产生影响。
transcript.english: So that's the kind of expression of passion that makes a difference while informing with respect to this question of inspiring.

transcript.chinese: 哦，是的，当然，在这个承诺阶段，你也可以表达东西有多酷。
transcript.english: Oh, yeah and of course, during this promise phase, you can also express how cool stuff is.

transcript.chinese: 让我举一个以这种方式开始的讲座的例子。
transcript.english: Let me give you an example of a lecture that starts this way.

transcript.chinese: 我说的是资源分配。
transcript.english: I'm talking about resource allocation.

transcript.chinese: 当你将飞机分配给航班时刻表或尝试安排工厂或类似的事情时，你会想到同样的东西。
transcript.english: It's the same sort of stuff you would think of when your-- it's the same sort of ideas you would need if you're allocating aircraft to a flight schedule or trying to schedule a factory or something like that.

transcript.chinese: 但这个例子是在美国的各州上涂上颜色，而没有任何接壤的州具有相同的颜色。
transcript.english: But the example is putting colors on the states in the United States without any bordering states having the same color.

transcript.chinese: 所以就这样了。
transcript.english: So here it goes.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我在课程开始时展示的内容。
transcript.english: This is what I show at the beginning of the class.

transcript.chinese: 这是一种进行着色的方法。
transcript.english: This is a way of doing that coloring.

transcript.chinese: 你可能会说，好吧，我们为什么不等到它完成呢？
transcript.english: And you might say, well, why don't we wait till it finishes?

transcript.chinese: 你愿意这样做吗？
transcript.english: Would you like to do that?

transcript.chinese: 不？
transcript.english: No?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我们不会等到它结束，因为在这个程序结束之前太阳就会爆炸并吞噬地球。 [笑声] 但是，只要稍微调整一下程序的运作方式，我告诉我的学生，你们在接下来的 50 分钟内就会明白，这就是你们所得到的。
transcript.english: Well, we're not going to wait till it finishes because the sun will have exploded and consumed the earth before this program finishes. [LAUGHTER] But with a slight adjustment to how the program works, which I tell my students you will understand in the next 50 minutes, this is what you get.

transcript.chinese: 这不是很酷吗？
transcript.english: Isn't that cool?

transcript.chinese: 你一定会对那些将比太阳系寿命还要长的计算时间缩短到几秒钟的东西感到惊讶。
transcript.english: You got to be amazed by stuff that takes a computation from longer than the lifetime of the solar system into a few seconds.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我所说的预先做出承诺并表达对你所谈论的事情的热情的意思。
transcript.english: So that's what I mean by providing a promise upfront and expressing some passion about what you're talking about.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，这个小块中的最后一项是与人们认为他们在麻省理工学院所做的事情有关。
transcript.english: Well, the last item in this little block here is it has to do with what people think that they do it at MIT.

transcript.chinese: 你问老师最重要的目的是什么，他们会说，好吧，我做的最重要的事情
transcript.english: You ask faculty what the most important purpose is, and they'll say, well, the most important thing I do

transcript.chinese: 就是教人们如何思考。
transcript.english: is teach people how to think.

transcript.chinese: 然后你说哦，那太好了。
transcript.english: And then you say oh, that's great.

transcript.chinese: 你如何教人们如何思考？
transcript.english: How do you teach people how to think?

transcript.chinese: 茫然的凝视。
transcript.english: Blank stare.

transcript.chinese: 没有人能完全回答这部分，也就是下一个自然问题。
transcript.english: No one can quite respond to that part, that natural next question.

transcript.chinese: 那么如何教人们如何思考呢？
transcript.english: So how do you teach people how to think?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，我相信我们是讲故事的动物。
transcript.english: Well, I believe that we are storytelling animals.

transcript.chinese: 我们在童年时就开始通过童话故事来发展我们的故事、理解和操纵技能，并继续在法律、商业、医学等专业学校学习。
transcript.english: And that we start developing our story, understanding and manipulating skills with fairy tales in childhood and continue on through professional schools like law, business, medicine, everything.

transcript.chinese: 我们一生都会继续这样做。
transcript.english: And we continue doing that throughout life.

transcript.chinese: 如果这就是思考的意义的话。
transcript.english: So if that is what thinking is all about.

transcript.chinese: 我们想教人们如何思考，你为他们提供他们需要知道的故事，他们需要针对这些故事提出的问题，分析这些故事的机制，将故事放在一起的方法，以及评估故事可靠性的方法。
transcript.english: And we want to teach people how to think, you provide them with the stories they need to know, the questions they need to ask about those stories, mechanisms for analyzing those stories, ways of putting stories together, ways of evaluating how reliable a story is.

transcript.chinese: 我认为这就是当你教人们如何思考时你需要做的。
transcript.english: And that's what I think you need to do when you teach people how to think.

transcript.chinese: 但这就是教育的全部内容。
transcript.english: But that's all about education.

transcript.chinese: 你们中的许多人不一定是为了这个，而是为了这一部分，为了说服，这分为几类，口试（未展示），行话，出名。
transcript.english: And many of you here are not necessarily for that, but rather for this part, for persuading, which breaks down into several categories, oral exams, not shown, shop talks, getting famous.

transcript.chinese: 我不会过多谈论口语考试，只是说口语考试过去比现在可怕得多。
transcript.english: I won't say much about oral exams other than the fact that they used to be a lot scarier than they are today.

transcript.chinese: 以前，读文献
transcript.english: In the old days, reading the literature

transcript.chinese: 外语是其中的一部分。
transcript.english: in a foreign language was a part of that.

transcript.chinese: 而且失败率很高。
transcript.english: And there was a high failure rate.

transcript.chinese: 当你回顾这些失败时，人们口语考试失败的最常见原因是未能定位和未能练习。
transcript.english: And when you look back on those failures, the most usual reason for people failing an oral exam is failure to situate and a failure to practice.

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是，在情境中谈论你的研究很重要。
transcript.english: By situate, I mean, it's important to talk about your research in context.

transcript.chinese: 这是全世界都在追求的问题。
transcript.english: This is a problem that's being pursued all over the world.

transcript.chinese: 过去30年我面前没有任何进步。
transcript.english: There hasn't been any progress before me in the past 30 years.

transcript.chinese: 每个人都在寻找解决方案，因为它会影响很多其他事情，例如情境、时间、地点和感觉。
transcript.english: Everyone is looking for a solution because it will have impact on so many other things, such situating and time and place and feel.

transcript.chinese: 然后就实践而言，是的，实践很重要。
transcript.english: And then as far as practice is concerned, yes, practice is important.

transcript.chinese: 但这并不意味着向与您共享的人展示您的幻灯片。
transcript.english: But that doesn't mean showing your slides to the people you share an with.

transcript.chinese: 这样做的问题是，如果人们知道你在做什么，他们就会产生幻觉，认为你的演示文稿中有一些材料，如果它不存在的话，那么这些材料就不存在。
transcript.english: The problem with that is that if people know what you're doing, they will hallucinate that there's material in your presentation that isn't there if it isn't there.

transcript.chinese: 顺便说一下，情况的一个变化是，你的导师并不是一个很好的人来帮助你调试演讲，因为他们实际上知道你在做什么。
transcript.english: A variation on the scene, by the way, is your faculty supervisor is not a very good person to help you debug a talk because they, in fact, know what you're doing.

transcript.chinese: 事实上，他们会产生幻觉
transcript.english: And they will, in fact, hallucinate

transcript.chinese: 您的演示文稿中存在一些不存在的材料。
transcript.english: there's material in your presentation that isn't there.

transcript.chinese: 所以你需要和一些不知道你在做什么的朋友聚在一起，让他们——好吧，你在练习开始时说，如果你不能让我哭，我就不再重视作为朋友了。 [笑声] 然后当你到学院参加口语考试时，
transcript.english: So you need to get together with some friends who don't know what you're doing and have them-- well, you start the practice session by saying, if you can't make me cry, I won't value as a friend anymore. [LAUGHTER] And then when you get to the faculty on a oral exam,

transcript.chinese: 这很容易。
transcript.english: it will be easy.

transcript.chinese: 你看，难度——你从某人那里受到的批评的程度与年龄成正比。
transcript.english: You see, difficulty-- the amount of flak you'll get from somebody is proportional to age.

transcript.chinese: 人年纪越大，就越了解自己在世界上的位置。
transcript.english: The older somebody is, the more they understand where they are in the world.

transcript.chinese: 但年轻人试图向老年人展示他们有多么聪明，所以这是微妙的恶毒。
transcript.english: But the young people are trying to show the old people how smart they are, so it's subtly vicious.

transcript.chinese: 因此，只要你有机会拥有一个由白发苍苍的人组成的审查委员会，这就是你想要的。
transcript.english: So whenever you have an opportunity to have an examining committee that's full of people with gray hair, that's what you want.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，这只是我未在此处列出的内容的一两句话。
transcript.english: Well, that's just a word or two about something I haven't listed here.

transcript.chinese: 让我们进入工作会谈的主题。
transcript.english: Let's get into the subject of job talks.

transcript.chinese: 很多年前，我坐在圣地亚哥的一家酒吧里。
transcript.english: So I was sitting in a bar many years ago in San Diego.

transcript.chinese: 我是海军科学委员会的成员，我和委员会的几位同事——来自科罗拉多大学的德洛丽丝·埃特（Delores Etter）坐在一起。
transcript.english: I was a member of the Navy Science Board, and I was sitting with a couple of my colleagues on the board Delores Etter from the University of Colorado.

transcript.chinese: 她让我嫉妒得要吐口水，因为她写了 21 本书，而我只写了 17 本书。
transcript.english: She made me so jealous I could spit because she'd written 21 books, and I'd only written 17.

transcript.chinese: 另一位是来自德克萨斯大学的比尔·韦尔登。
transcript.english: And then the other one was Bill Weldon from the University of Texas.

transcript.chinese: 他是一个电磁学专家，而且他
transcript.english: He was an electromagnetism guy, and he

transcript.chinese: 知道如何使用轨道炮驱动钢棒穿过坦克装甲。
transcript.english: knew how to use rail guns to drive steel rods through tank armor.

transcript.chinese: 这些都是有趣的人。
transcript.english: These were interesting people.

transcript.chinese: 所以我说，你在教师候选人中寻找什么？
transcript.english: So I said, what do you look for in a faculty candidate?

transcript.chinese: 德洛丽丝说，在一微秒之内，他们必须向我们展示他们有某种愿景，比尔紧随其后说，他们必须向我们展示他们已经做了一些事情。
transcript.english: And within one microsecond, Delores said, they have to show us they've got some kind of vision, quickly followed by Bill who said, they have to show us that they've done something.

transcript.chinese: 哦，听起来不错，我说。
transcript.english: Oh, that sounds good, I said.

transcript.chinese: 然后我对他们说，候选人需要多长时间才能确定这两件事？
transcript.english: And then I said to them, how long does a candidate have to establish these two things?

transcript.chinese: 你怎么认为？
transcript.english: What do you think?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，将你的答案与他们的答案进行比较。
transcript.english: Well, compare your answer to theirs.

transcript.chinese: 五分钟。
transcript.english: Five minutes.

transcript.chinese: 所以，如果你没有表达你的愿景，如果你没有告诉人们你在五分钟内做了某件事，你就已经输了。
transcript.english: So if you haven't expressed your vision, if you haven't told people that you've done something in five minutes, you've already lost.

transcript.chinese: 所以你必须能够做到这一点。
transcript.english: So you have to be able to do that.

transcript.chinese: 让我提一下与此相关的几件事。
transcript.english: And let me just mention a couple of things in that connection.

transcript.chinese: 在这里，愿景在一定程度上是某人关心的问题和你的方法中的一些新东西。
transcript.english: Here, the vision is in part, a problem that somebody cares about and something new in your approach.

transcript.chinese: 所以问题是理解人类智能的本质。
transcript.english: So the problem is understanding the nature of human intelligence.

transcript.chinese: 这种方法是询问我们与黑猩猩和尼安德特人的不同之处。
transcript.english: And the approach is asking questions about what makes us different from chimpanzees and Neanderthals.

transcript.chinese: 这仅仅是数量的问题，还是我们只是在某种持续的方式上变得更聪明了一点？
transcript.english: Is it merely a matter of quantity, or we're just a little bit smarter in some continuous way?

transcript.chinese: 或者我们是否拥有一些黑猩猩和尼安德特人所没有的根本不同的东西？
transcript.english: Or do we have something that's fundamentally different that chimpanzees don't have and Neanderthals either?

transcript.chinese: 答案是肯定的，我们确实有一些不同的东西。
transcript.english: And the answer is yes, we do have something different.

transcript.chinese: 我们是象征性的生物。
transcript.english: We are symbolic creatures.

transcript.chinese: 因为我们是象征性的生物，所以我们可以对关系和事件建立象征性的描述。
transcript.english: And because we're symbolic creatures, we can build symbolic descriptions of relations and events.

transcript.chinese: 我们可以把它们串在一起编故事。
transcript.english: We can string them together and make stories.

transcript.chinese: 因为我们可以创造故事，所以我们与众不同。
transcript.english: And because we can make stories, that's what makes us different.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我的巡回演讲。
transcript.english: So that's my stump speech.

transcript.chinese: 这就是我大部分关于我个人研究的演讲的开始方式。
transcript.english: That's how I start most of my talks on my own personal research.

transcript.chinese: 你如何表达你已经做了某事的想法？
transcript.english: How do you express the notion that you've done something?

transcript.chinese: 列出解决该问题所需采取的步骤。
transcript.english: By listing the steps that need to be taken in order to achieve the solution to that problem.

transcript.chinese: 您不必完成所有这些步骤。
transcript.english: You don't have to have done all of those steps.

transcript.chinese: 但你可以说这就是需要做的事情。
transcript.english: But you can say here's what needs to be done.

transcript.chinese: 举个例子，这就是需要做的事情。
transcript.english: An example, here's what needs to be done.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要指定一些行为。
transcript.english: We need to specify some behavior.

transcript.chinese: 我们需要枚举能够处理该行为的约束。
transcript.english: We need to enumerate the constraints that make it possible to deal with that behavior.

transcript.chinese: 我们必须实施一个系统，因为我们是工程师，除非我们能够构建它，否则我们不认为我们已经理解了某些东西。
transcript.english: We have to implement a system because we're engineers, and we don't think that we've understood something unless we can build it.

transcript.chinese: 我们已经建立了这样一个系统，并且我们
transcript.english: And we've built such a system, and we're

transcript.chinese: 今天就给大家演示一下。
transcript.english: about to demonstrate it to you today.

transcript.chinese: 这将是列举实现愿景所需的一系列步骤的示例。
transcript.english: That would be an example of enumerating a series of steps needed to realize the vision.

transcript.chinese: 然后，等等，等等，等等，等等，等等，等等。
transcript.english: So then blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah.

transcript.chinese: 然后你通过列举你的贡献来结束。
transcript.english: And then you conclude by-- you conclude by enumerating your contributions.

transcript.chinese: 它是这些步骤的镜像。
transcript.english: It's kind of mirror of these steps.

transcript.chinese: 它有助于确定你已经做了某事。
transcript.english: And it helps to establish that you've done something.

transcript.chinese: 这是一种用于进行技术演讲的通用框架。
transcript.english: So that's a kind of general purpose framework for doing a technical talk.

transcript.chinese: 现在，今天只剩下几件事要做了。
transcript.english: Now, only a few more things left to do today.

transcript.chinese: 出名是我们议程上的下一个项目，因为一旦你得到这份工作，你需要考虑一下你的工作将如何得到认可。
transcript.english: Getting famous is the next item on our agenda because once you've got the job, you need to think a little bit about how you're going to be recognized for what you do.

transcript.chinese: 所以哦，首先，你为什么要关心出名呢？
transcript.english: So oh, first of all, why should you care about getting famous?

transcript.chinese: 我想到这一点与我参加过一次的筹款活动有关，这是一次筹款活动，旨在筹集资金以拯救威尼斯免遭水下和所有艺术品被毁。
transcript.english: I thought about this in connection with a fundraising event I attended once, a fundraising event for raising money to save Venice from going under water and having all of its art destroyed.

transcript.chinese: 无论如何，我坐在这里，JC 也坐在这里。
transcript.english: Anyway, I was sitting here, and JC was sitting here.

transcript.chinese: 那就是朱莉娅，已故的朱莉娅·柴尔德。
transcript.english: That was Julia, the late Julia Child.

transcript.chinese: 随着夜晚的过去，越来越多的人会过来请朱莉娅签名，或者表达她改变了他们的生活的感觉。
transcript.english: And as the evening wore on, more and more people would come up and ask Julia to autograph something or express a feeling that she had changed their life.

transcript.chinese: 而且这种事一而再、再而三地发生。
transcript.english: And it just happened over and over again.

transcript.chinese: 所以最终，我转向朱莉娅，我说，
transcript.english: So eventually, I turned to Julia, and I said, Ms.

transcript.chinese: 女士孩子，出名很有趣吗？
transcript.english: Child, is it fun to be famous?

transcript.chinese: 她想了一会儿。
transcript.english: And she thought about it for a second.

transcript.chinese: 她说，你习惯了。 [笑声] 但你知道我发生了什么吗？
transcript.english: And she said, you get used to it. [LAUGHTER] But you know what occured to me?

transcript.chinese: 你永远不会习惯被忽视。
transcript.english: You never get used to being ignored.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是——这是一种思考方式。
transcript.english: So it's-- here's a way to think about it.

transcript.chinese: 你的想法就像你的孩子。
transcript.english: Your ideas are like your children.

transcript.chinese: 你也不希望他们衣衫褴褛地来到这个世界。
transcript.english: And you don't want them to go into the world in rags.

transcript.chinese: 因此，你要做的就是确保你拥有这些技术、这些机制、这些关于如何表达你的想法的想法，以便它们的价值得到认可。
transcript.english: So what you want to do is to be sure that you have these techniques, these mechanisms, these thoughts about how to present ideas that you have so that they're recognized for the value that is in them.

transcript.chinese: 这就是为什么关注包装是合理的事情。
transcript.english: So that's why it's a legitimate thing to concern yourself with packaging.

transcript.chinese: 现在，你如何被记住？
transcript.english: Now, how do you get remembered?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，我喜欢称之为温斯顿的明星。
transcript.english: Well, there's something I like to call Winston's star.

transcript.chinese: 我要阐述的每一项都以 S 开头。
transcript.english: And every one of the items I'm about to articulate starts with an S.

transcript.chinese: 因此，如果您希望您的演示想法被记住，您需要做的事情之一就是确保您有某种与您的作品相关的符号。
transcript.english: So if you want your presentation ideas to be remembered, one of the things you need to do is to make sure that you have some kind of symbol associated with your work.

transcript.chinese: 所以这个拱形例子实际上来自我很多很多年前的博士论文。
transcript.english: So this arch example is actually from my PhD thesis many, many years ago.

transcript.chinese: 而在我当时的工作过程中，
transcript.english: And in the course of my work at that time,

transcript.chinese: 这部关于拱门学习的作品变得有点名气，我不知道为什么。
transcript.english: this work on arch learning became mildly famous, and I didn't know why.

transcript.chinese: 多年以后我才发现，那部作品无意间拥有了这颗星星上的所有元素。
transcript.english: It was only many years later that I realized that that work accidentally had all of the elements on this star.

transcript.chinese: 所以第一个要素是有一种象征。
transcript.english: So the first element is that there was a kind of symbol.

transcript.chinese: 这是拱门本身。
transcript.english: It's the arch itself.

transcript.chinese: 接下来你需要的是某种口号，一种可以指导工作的短语。
transcript.english: Next thing you need is some kind of slogan, a kind of phrase that provides a handle on the work.

transcript.chinese: 在这种情况下，这句话是一次性学习的。
transcript.english: And in this case, the phrase was one shot learning.

transcript.chinese: 这是一次尝试，因为我编写的程序从呈现给我们的每个示例中学到了一些明确的东西。
transcript.english: And it was one shot because the program I wrote learned something definite from every example that was presented to us.

transcript.chinese: 因此，在从基于此配置的模型转变为不是基于该配置的架构的模型时，程序了解到它必须处于最顶层，一次性学习。
transcript.english: So in going from a model based on this configuration to something that isn't an arch base on that configuration, the program learned that it has to be on top, one shot learning.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是一个象征、口号。
transcript.english: So that's a symbol, slogan.

transcript.chinese: 现在我们需要一个惊喜。
transcript.english: And now we need a surprise.

transcript.chinese: 是的，令人惊讶的是你不需要一百万个例子来学习。
transcript.english: Yeah, the surprise is you don't need a million examples of something to learn.

transcript.chinese: 如果您足够聪明，可以适当地利用该示例，那么您可以通过一个示例来完成此操作。
transcript.english: You can do it with one example if you're smart enough to make use of that example appropriately.

transcript.chinese: 这就是惊喜。
transcript.english: So that was the surprise.

transcript.chinese: 你可以从每个例子中学到一些明确的东西。
transcript.english: You can learn something definite from each example.

transcript.chinese: 下一个项目是一个突出的想法。
transcript.english: Next item was a salient idea.

transcript.chinese: 现在，当我说突出的想法时，我并不是说重要。
transcript.english: Now, when I say salient idea, I don't mean important.

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是一个突出的想法。
transcript.english: What I mean is an idea that sticks out.

transcript.chinese: 有趣的是，有些论文有太多好主意，你不知道它到底讲了什么，因为它是哪一篇？
transcript.english: Some theses, funnily enough, have too many good ideas, and you don't know what it's all about because which one is it?

transcript.chinese: 所以你需要一个突出的想法。
transcript.english: So you need an idea that sticks out.

transcript.chinese: 这里突出的想法是“险些失手”的概念。
transcript.english: And the idea that stuck out here was the notion of a near miss.

transcript.chinese: 你看，这不是一个拱门，但它并没有偏离太多。
transcript.english: You see, this is not an arch, but it doesn't miss by much.

transcript.chinese: 所以这是一次千钧一发的失败。
transcript.english: So it's a near miss.

transcript.chinese: 最后，你需要讲述你是如何做到的、它是如何运作的、为什么它很重要。
transcript.english: And finally, you need to tell the story of how you did it, how it works, why it's important.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就是关于如何不那么出名，而是如何确保你的工作得到认可。
transcript.english: So that's a bit on how to not so much get famous, but how to ensure that your work is recognized.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我们快要完成了，因为现在我们要讨论最后一项，即如何停止。
transcript.english: Well, we're almost finished because now we're down to this last item, which is how to stop.

transcript.chinese: 当我们谈到这个时，有一个问题：好吧，最后一张幻灯片是什么？
transcript.english: And when we come to that, there's a question of all right, well, what is the final slide?

transcript.chinese: 最后的话是什么？
transcript.english: And what are the final words?

transcript.chinese: 因此，在最后一张幻灯片中，让我给您提供一些可能性的示例。
transcript.english: So for the final slide, let me give you some examples of possibilities.

transcript.chinese: 这个怎么样？
transcript.english: How about this one?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，您可能会看到这张幻灯片并想到，麻省理工学院有 1,000 名教职员工。
transcript.english: Well, you might see that slide and think to yourself, there are 1,000 faculty at MIT.

transcript.chinese: 不错的工作，但不是那么多，但如果除以 1,000，这只是一个很小的工作。
transcript.english: Nice piece of work, but not so much, but it's only a tiny piece of work if you divide by 1,000.

transcript.chinese: 因此，当你在演讲结束时展示一整套庞大的合作者名单时，你会感到有点失望，因为这表明没人知道。
transcript.english: So when you show a whole gigantic list of collaborators at the end of a talk, it's a kind of let down because it suggests that nobody knows.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，你做了什么有意义的事情吗？
transcript.english: Well, did you do anything significant?

transcript.chinese: 现在，你必须认出你的合作者，对吗？
transcript.english: Now, you've got to recognize your collaborators, right?

transcript.chinese: 那么你在哪里做呢？
transcript.english: So where do you do that?

transcript.chinese: 不是在最后一张幻灯片上，而是在第一张幻灯片上。
transcript.english: Not on the last slide, on the first slide.

transcript.chinese: 所有这些都在第一张幻灯片上。
transcript.english: All this was on the first slide.

transcript.chinese: 这些是合作者，所以你不想把他们放在最后。
transcript.english: These are the collaborators, so you don't want to put them at the end.

transcript.chinese: 你不想要这样的幻灯片。
transcript.english: You don't want a slide like this.

transcript.chinese: 这个怎么样？
transcript.english: How about this one?

transcript.chinese: 这是结束谈话的最糟糕的方式。 [笑声] 因为这张幻灯片可以在那里放 20 分钟。
transcript.english: This is the worst possible way to end a talk. [LAUGHTER] Because this slide can be up there for 20 minutes.

transcript.chinese: 我已经看到它发生了。
transcript.english: I've seen it happen.

transcript.chinese: 它浪费房地产。
transcript.english: It squanders real estate.

transcript.chinese: 它浪费了告诉人们你是谁的机会。
transcript.english: It squanders an opportunity to tell people who you are.

transcript.chinese: 只是——这个怎么样？
transcript.english: It's just--  What about this one?

transcript.chinese: 我经常看到它。
transcript.english: I often see it.

transcript.chinese: 我从来没见过有人把它写下来。
transcript.english: I never see anybody write it down.

transcript.chinese: 而且，这也浪费了机会。
transcript.english: Also, it wastes opportunity.

transcript.chinese: 天啊，更糟糕的是。
transcript.english: Oh my God, even worse.

transcript.chinese: 所有这些行对你都没有任何作用。
transcript.english: All of these lines do nothing for you.

transcript.chinese: 他们浪费了你告诉人们的机会——让人们知道你是什么——你是谁。
transcript.english: They waste an opportunity for you to tell people-- to leave people with what you-- with who you are.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，那这个呢？
transcript.english: Well, what about this?

transcript.chinese: 这是一件好事吗？
transcript.english: Is this a good one?

transcript.chinese: 乍一看似乎是这样，但问题就在这里。
transcript.english: It might seem so at first, but here's the problem.

transcript.chinese: 如果你说这些是我的结论，那么这些都是完全合理的结论，没有人关心。
transcript.english: If you say these are my conclusions, these are perfectly legitimate conclusions that nobody cares about.

transcript.chinese: 他们关心的是你做了什么。
transcript.english: What they care about is what you have done.

transcript.chinese: 这就是为什么你的最后一张幻灯片应该有这个标签：贡献。
transcript.english: And that's why your final slide should have this label, contributions.

transcript.chinese: 这反映了我在那里所说的工作会谈应该像三明治一样。
transcript.english: It's a mirror of what I said over there about how job talks ought to be like a sandwich.

transcript.chinese: 最后一张幻灯片，即人们提出问题并提交时显示的幻灯片，它应该是有您贡献的幻灯片。
transcript.english: And the final slide, the one that's up there while people are asking questions and filing out, it ought to be the one that has your contributions on it.

transcript.chinese: 这是我自己的演讲中的一个例子。
transcript.english: Here's an example from my own stump speech.

transcript.chinese: 是的，这就是我经常谈论的话题。
transcript.english: Yeah, this is what I talk about a lot.

transcript.chinese: 是的，这是我通常展示的内容。
transcript.english: Yes, here are the things that I typically demonstrate.

transcript.chinese: 我等待人们阅读它。
transcript.english: And I wait for people to read it.

transcript.chinese: 最后一个元素是我们从中得到的，所以这是贡献幻灯片的一个例子。
transcript.english: And the final element there is this is what we get out of it, so that's an example of a contribution slide.

transcript.chinese: 好吧，现在，其他部分呢？
transcript.english: All right, now, what about the other part?

transcript.chinese: 你的最后一张幻灯片就在那里。
transcript.english: You got your final slide up there.

transcript.chinese: 这是一张贡献幻灯片。
transcript.english: It's a contribution slide.

transcript.chinese: 你必须以某种方式告诉人们你已经完成了。
transcript.english: Somehow you have to tell people you're finished.

transcript.chinese: 让我们看看，看看几种可能性。
transcript.english: So let's see it, check out a few possibilities.

transcript.chinese: 在最后一句话中你可以做的一件事就是你可以讲一个笑话。
transcript.english: One thing you could do in the final words is you could tell a joke.

transcript.chinese: 没关系。
transcript.english: It's OK.

transcript.chinese: 当您完成时，人们已经根据您的声音参数进行了调整。
transcript.english: By the time you're done, people have adjusted themselves to your voice parameters.

transcript.chinese: 他们已经准备好开玩笑了。
transcript.english: They're ready for a joke.

transcript.chinese: 我和一位名叫道格·莱纳特的同事坐在德克萨斯州奥斯汀的另一家酒吧里。
transcript.english: I was sitting in another bar, this time in Austin, Texas with a colleague of mine named Doug Lenat.

transcript.chinese: 道格是一位出色的演讲者。
transcript.english: And Doug's a fantastic speaker.

transcript.chinese: 所以我对道格说，道格，你是一位出色的演讲者，你的秘诀是什么？
transcript.english: And so I said to Doug, Doug, you're a fantastic speaker, what's your secret?

transcript.chinese: 他说，哦，我总是以一个笑话结束，这样，人们就会认为他们一直玩得很开心。
transcript.english: And he said, oh, I always finish with a joke, and that way, people think they've had fun the whole time.

transcript.chinese: [笑声]所以是的，一个笑话会在那里发挥作用。
transcript.english: [LAUGHTER] So yeah, a joke will work down there.

transcript.chinese: 这个怎么样？
transcript.english: How about this one?

transcript.chinese: 谢谢。
transcript.english: Thank you.

transcript.chinese: 我不推荐它。
transcript.english: I don't recommend it.

transcript.chinese: 这是一个软弱的举动。
transcript.english: It's a weak move.

transcript.chinese: 如果你以感谢来结束你的演讲，你不会下地狱，但这是一个软弱的举动，原因如下。
transcript.english: You will not go to hell if you conclude your talk by saying thank you, but it's a weak move, and here's why.

transcript.chinese: 当你说“谢谢”时，更糟糕的是，“谢谢你的倾听”，这表明每个人出于礼貌而留了那么久，并且他们非常渴望去别的地方。
