transcript.english: When you say thank you, even worse, thank you for listening, it suggests that everybody has stayed that long out of politeness and that they had a profound desire to be somewhere else.

transcript.chinese: 但他们很有礼貌，所以坚持了下来。
transcript.english: But they're so polite, they stuck it out.

transcript.chinese: 这就是你要感谢他们的原因。
transcript.english: And that's what you're thanking them for.

transcript.chinese: 所以一旦开始热烈的掌声，你就可以说声谢谢，这没有什么错。
transcript.english: So once wild applause has started, you can mouth a thank you, and there's nothing wrong with that.

transcript.chinese: 但你做的最后一件事不应该是说谢谢。
transcript.english: But the last thing you do should not be saying thank you.

transcript.chinese: 现在，你对我说，好吧，不是每个人都说谢谢吗？
transcript.english: Now, you say to me, well, doesn't everybody say thank you?

transcript.chinese: 嗯，每个人做的事不一定都是正确的。
transcript.english: Well, what everybody does is not necessarily the right thing.

transcript.chinese: 我想说明一些谈话如何在不说谢谢的情况下就结束了。
transcript.english: And I like to illustrate how some talks can end without saying thank you.

transcript.chinese: 我喜欢从政治演讲中汲取灵感，但我最近听到的那些演讲不太好，所以——[笑声]所以我必须回去一点。
transcript.english: I like to draw from political speeches, but the ones that I've heard recently aren't so good, so-- [LAUGHTER] So I'm going to have to go go back a little bit.

transcript.chinese: 这是克里斯蒂州长。
transcript.english: So here is Governor Christie.

transcript.chinese: 他发表了一年的共和党主题演讲。
transcript.english: He gave the Republican keynote address one year.

transcript.chinese: 他的演讲到此结束。
transcript.english: This is the end of his talk.

transcript.chinese: 让我们看看他做了什么。 [掌声] 克里斯·克里斯蒂： 大家一起，一起。
transcript.english: Let's see what he does. [APPLAUSE] CHRIS CHRISTIE: And together, everybody, together.

transcript.chinese: 我们将再次为我们的子孙后代维护美国的伟大。
transcript.english: We will stand up once again for American greatness for our children and grandchildren.

transcript.chinese: 上帝保佑你们，上帝保佑美国。 [掌声] 帕特里克·温斯顿： 这是一个经典的祝福结局。
transcript.english: God bless you, and God bless America. [APPLAUSE] PATRICK WINSTON: So that's a classic benediction ending.

transcript.chinese: 上帝保佑你，上帝保佑美国。
transcript.english: God bless you, God bless America.

transcript.chinese: 现在，我不想在这件事上有党派之争。
transcript.english: Now, I don't want to be partisan about this.

transcript.chinese: 所以我想我最好转向在民主党全国代表大会上发表主题演讲。
transcript.english: So I think I'd better switch to the keynote address in the Democratic Convention.

transcript.chinese: 那一年，比尔·克林顿 (Bill Clinton) 发表了这句话，他对如何演讲有所了解。
transcript.english: It was delivered that year by Bill Clinton, who knows something about how to speak.

transcript.chinese: [掌声]比尔·克林顿：如果这就是你想要的，如果这就是你所相信的，你必须投票，你必须再次选举巴拉克·奥巴马总统。
transcript.english: [APPLAUSE] BILL CLINTON: If that is what you want, if that is what you believe, you must vote, and you must re-elect President Barack Obama.

transcript.chinese: 上帝保佑你们，上帝保佑美国。
transcript.english: God bless you, and God bless America.

transcript.chinese: [掌声][笑声]
transcript.english: [APPLAUSE] [LAUGHTER]

transcript.chinese: 帕特里克·温斯顿：现在，看这个。
transcript.english: PATRICK WINSTON: Now, watch this.

transcript.chinese: 让我们回过头来重做一下。
transcript.english: Let's go back a little bit and redo it.

transcript.chinese: 我想让你看到的是，有一刻，他似乎几乎闭上了嘴唇，强迫自己不要说谢谢。
transcript.english: What I want you to see is that at one point, he seems to be almost pressing his lips together, forcing himself not to say thank you.

transcript.chinese: 然后还有一个地方他稍微行了个礼。
transcript.english: Then there's another place where he does a little salute.

transcript.chinese: 所以这次要留意那些。 [掌声]比尔·克林顿：如果这就是你想要的，如果这就是你所相信的，你必须投票，你必须再次选举巴拉克·奥巴马总统。
transcript.english: So watch for those this time around. [APPLAUSE] BILL CLINTON: If that is what you want, if that is what you believe, you must vote, and you must re-elect President Barack Obama.

transcript.chinese: 上帝保佑你，上帝保佑美国。
transcript.english: God bless you., and God bless America.

transcript.chinese: [掌声] 帕特里克·温斯顿： 这就是他噘起嘴唇的地方。 [笑声] 敬礼了。
transcript.english: [APPLAUSE] PATRICK WINSTON: That's where he's pursing his lips. [LAUGHTER] There's the salute.

transcript.chinese: [笑声] 是的，我认为这非常好。
transcript.english: [LAUGHTER] Yeah, I think that's pretty good.

transcript.chinese: 现在，我们要从中得到什么？
transcript.english: Now, what are we gonna take away from this?

transcript.chinese: 好吧，我想我可以通过说上帝保佑你，上帝保佑麻省理工学院来结束这次演讲，但它可能不会那么有效。
transcript.english: Well, I suppose I could conclude this talk by saying God bless you, and God bless the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, but it might not work so well.

transcript.chinese: 但你能从中得到的是你不必说谢谢。
transcript.english: But what you can get out of this is you don't have to say thank you.

transcript.chinese: 您还可以做其他事情。
transcript.english: There are other things you can do.

transcript.chinese: 有趣的是，随着时间的推移，人们明白了这一点，并且有一些结束事情的固定方法。
transcript.english: And it's interesting that over time, people figure this out, and there's some stock ways of ending things.

transcript.chinese: 因此，在天主教堂和古老的拉丁弥撒中，弥撒以ite missa est结束，大致翻译为“好吧，弥撒结束了，你现在可以回家了”。 [笑声]当然，在音乐会上，你知道不是在歌曲结束时鼓掌，而是在指挥走过去与音乐会主持人握手时鼓掌。
transcript.english: So in the Catholic church, and the good old Latin mass, it ended with ite missa est, which translates approximately to OK, the mass is over, you can go home now. [LAUGHTER] And of course, at musical concerts, you know that it's time to clap not at the end of the song, but rather when the conductor goes over and shakes hands with the concert master.

transcript.chinese: 这些惯例告诉您事件已经结束。
transcript.english: Those are conventions that tell you that the event is over.

transcript.chinese: 所以这些都是这里的可能性。
transcript.english: So those are all possibilities for here.

transcript.chinese: 但还有一种可能，那就是你可以向观众致敬。
transcript.english: But one more possibility, and that is that you can salute the audience.

transcript.chinese: 我的意思是，你可以说出你在某个地方有多珍惜你的时间。
transcript.english: And by that, I mean, you can say something about how much you value your time at a place.

transcript.chinese: 所以我可以说，嗯，来这里很有趣。
transcript.english: So I could say, well, it's been great fun being here.

transcript.chinese: 看到你们在麻省理工学院所做的事情真是令人着迷。
transcript.english: It's been fascinating to see what you folks are doing here at MIT.

transcript.chinese: 你提出的各种问题让我受到很大的刺激和激发，这真的很棒。
transcript.english: I've been much stimulated and provoked by the kinds of questions you've been asking, it's been really great.

transcript.chinese: 我期待着将来多次回来。
transcript.english: And I look forward to coming back on many occasions in the future.

transcript.chinese: 所以这就向观众致敬。
transcript.english: So that salutes the audience.

transcript.chinese: 你可以这么做。
transcript.english: You can do that.

transcript.chinese: 嗯，就是这样。
transcript.english: Well, there it is.

transcript.chinese: 你知道吗？
transcript.english: You know what?

transcript.chinese: 我很高兴你在这里。
transcript.english: I'm glad you're here.

transcript.chinese: 原因是通过来到这里，我认为你已经表现出一种理解，即如何表达和如何包装你的想法是一件重要的事情。
transcript.english: And the reason is by being here, I think you have demonstrated an understanding that how you present and how you package your ideas is an important thing.

transcript.chinese: 我为此向你致敬。 [笑声]我建议你再回来带上你的朋友。 [掌声]
transcript.english: And I salute you for that. [LAUGHTER] And I suggest that you come back again and bring your friends. [APPLAUSE]
